channel.title: AI Mentions in Congressional Press Releases
channel.description: Press releases from members of Congress mentioning AI, artificial intelligence, or data center.

item.title: Ahead Of World Quantum Day, Senator Gillibrand Tours The Innovare Advancement Center
item.author: Kirsten E. Gillibrand
item.description:
Democrat · NY · Senate

…ir Force Research Laboratory (AFRL/RI). It fosters collaboration between government, academia, and industry to drive innovation in national security, Artificial Intelligence, and quantum sciences, while fostering STEM education. Senator Gillibrand highlighted thevital role the Griffiss Institute plays in Upstate New York…

item.title: ICYMI—Hagerty Joins Mornings with Maria on Fox Business to Discuss Iran, Reconciliation, AI
item.author: Bill Hagerty
item.description:
Republican · TN · Senate

…ttee and former U.S. Ambassador to Japan, joined Mornings with Maria on Fox Business to discuss Iran, reconciliation, Federal Reserve leadership, and artificial intelligence. Partial Transcript Hagerty on Iran and other revisionist nations: “It’s at least good to see other nations step in and try to offer a hand here. R…

…mum employment mandate. I think we’ll get back to basics. The Fed will be the engine that it needs to be to support our economic growth.” Hagerty on AI and financial system risks: “I think what we’ve seen is that Secretary Bessent is on it. He’s on target, and he’s making certain, as this new technol…

item.title: Wyden Urges House Members to Reject Handing Donald Trump Unchecked Surveillance Authority, Insist on Reforms to FISA Section 702
item.author: Ron Wyden
item.description:
Democrat · OR · Senate

…allows the government to buy Americans’ location data without a warrant. Current law has no protections against feeding purchased location data into AI systems to conduct unprecedented mass surveillance, and FBI Director Kash Patel admitted in an open Senate Intelligence Committee hearing this spring…

item.title: ICYMI: Hickenlooper Travels to Western Slope, Front Range to Talk Science, Workforce, Cost of Living Crisis, Health Care
item.author: John W. Hickenlooper
item.description:
Democrat · CO · Senate

…elled to Golden for a roundtable discussion at Colorado School of Mines with local officials, school leaders, students, and industry professionals on artificial intelligence (AI) and its applications. Hickenlooper focused on harnessing AI so it benefits everyone, while also preparing our workforce for the AI future. He sp…

…ndtable discussion at Colorado School of Mines with local officials, school leaders, students, and industry professionals on artificial intelligence (AI) and its applications. Hickenlooper focused on harnessing AI so it benefits everyone, while also preparing our workforce for the AI future. He specif…

item.title: SCAMS ALERT: Bipartisan Leaders of the Joint Economic Committee Warn Americans About Tax Season Scams
item.author: Donald S. Beyer, Jr.
item.description:
Democrat · VA · House

…ssued a bipartisan alert today warning Americans about common tax season scams. These scams have become increasingly common, especially with emerging AI tactics, and nearly one in four Americans have reported being victimized by a tax scam. “Criminal enterprises are exploiting tax season to target Am…

…ax service or go directly to IRS.gov.” “As Americans file their taxes this month, scammers are deploying an onslaught of attacks – often enhanced by artificial intelligence – designed to steal people’s money,” said Senator Hassan. “I encourage all taxpayers to review the tips in this bipartisan scams alert so that they c…

item.title: Landsman Leads New Bill Requiring Big Tech to Pay for Data Centers and No NDAs
item.author: Greg Landsman
item.description:
Democrat · OH · House

…incinnati, OH – Congressman Greg Landsman (D-OH-01) introduced a bill to ensure families and communities are protected from the growing concerns over Artificial Intelligence (AI) data centers. The rising demands of AI data centers are contributing to higher electricity costs for working families, posing environmental ris…

…an Greg Landsman (D-OH-01) introduced a bill to ensure families and communities are protected from the growing concerns over Artificial Intelligence (AI) data centers. The rising demands of AI data centers are contributing to higher electricity costs for working families, posing environmental risks,…

item.title: Tonko Introduces Power for the People Act to Shield Consumers from Rising Energy Costs
item.author: Paul Tonko
item.description:
Democrat · NY · House

…WASHINGTON, DC — Congressman Paul D. Tonko (NY-20) today introduced the Power for the People Act in the House, legislation that ensures data centers are held responsible for the high costs of their electricity demand. Senator Van Hollen (D- MD) has introduced companion legislation in the Senate.…

…sponsible for the high costs of their electricity demand. Senator Van Hollen (D- MD) has introduced companion legislation in the Senate. The rise of artificial intelligence models has resulted in data center development exploding in recent years. With data centers critical to national security, economic development, and…

item.title: Blumenthal Presses American Sports Leagues on Gambling & Prediction Market Partnerships
item.author: Richard Blumenthal
item.description:
Democrat · CT · Senate

…(D-NY). The SAFE Bet Act requires states offering sports betting to meet minimum federal standards in the categories of marketing, affordability and Artificial Intelligence to create a safer, less addictive product. Blumenthal also reintroduced the Gambling addiction Recovery, Investment, and Treatment (GRIT) Act, with U…

Republican · IA · Senate

Meta, Amazon AI Services, TikTok, Snapchat, Discord, X.AI, Grindr and Roblox in 2025 submitted over 17 million reports of suspected online child ex…

item.title: Cruz, Klobuchar TAKE IT DOWN Act Leads to Conviction in Case Targeting AI-Generated Deepfakes
item.author: Amy Klobuchar
item.description:
Democrat · MN · Senate

…) and Amy Klobuchar (D-MN) highlighted the first conviction under their bipartisan TAKE IT DOWN Act following the guilty plea of an Ohio man who used artificial intelligence to create and distribute non-consensual intimate images. “The Take It Down Act, which I authored with Senator Klobuchar, is instrumental in ensuring…

…crimes will be held accountable.” The bipartisan TAKE IT DOWN Act criminalizes the publication of non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII), including AI-generated NCII, and requires online platforms to implement procedures to remove such content within 48 hours of notice from a victim or their represe…

item.title: Fitzpatrick, Kelly Lead Bipartisan, Bicameral Letter Backing AFL-CIO AI Principles to Protect Workers
item.author: Brian K. Fitzpatrick
item.description:
Republican · PA · House

…Lawmakers urge balanced federal approach to AI that promotes innovation while safeguarding workers. WASHINGTON, DC—Today, Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick (R-PA-01) and Senator Mark Kelly (D-AZ) cal…

…ressman Brian Fitzpatrick (R-PA-01) and Senator Mark Kelly (D-AZ) called on the Administration to incorporate worker-centered principles into federal artificial intelligence (AI) policy, guidance, and procurement. As AI technologies are rapidly deployed across industries, the lawmakers highlighted the AFL-CIO’s AI princip…

item.title: Senator Coons statement on Anthropic’s launch of Project Glasswing
item.author: Christopher A. Coons
item.description:
Democrat · DE · Senate

…n response to Anthropic’s launch of Project Glasswing – a coalition of America’s leading technology companies working together to deploy cutting-edge AI models to find and fix dangerous security flaws in the software that underpins our health care system, our financial services, and our basic digital…

…Korea, and now increasingly Iran, are targeting our basic digital infrastructure, our financial services, and health care systems – and they’re using AI to do it. It highlights what I’ve seen as the ranking Democrat for defense appropriations: any country that gains an edge in the AI race will have a…

item.title: Warner Statement on Anthropic's Project Glasswing
item.author: Mark R. Warner
item.description:
Democrat · VA · Senate

…ring information, capabilities, and computing capacity to better protect our critical infrastructure. We are already seeing cyber threat actors using AI tools to improve their capabilities, putting government, businesses, and consumers’ security and personal information at risk. I have long worked to…

…formation at risk. I have long worked to shore up the cybersecurity of our critical infrastructure, from hospitals to government systems and more. As AI dramatically accelerates the discovery of new vulnerabilities, I hope industry will correspondingly accelerate and reprioritize patching.” ###…

item.title: Warren, Wyden Probe Third-Party Vendors on Disruptions to Unemployment Benefits Putting Americans’ Livelihood at Risk
item.author: Elizabeth Warren
item.description:
Democrat · MA · Senate

…eneficiaries’ UI. Vendors often provide states with a one-size-fits-all system, giving them little say in its operation. They have also begun to use artificial intelligence (AI) to help determine eligibility for UI, often using tools that reportedly struggle to understand nuances in claimants’ paperwork. This had led to…

…s often provide states with a one-size-fits-all system, giving them little say in its operation. They have also begun to use artificial intelligence (AI) to help determine eligibility for UI, often using tools that reportedly struggle to understand nuances in claimants’ paperwork. item.title: DelBene Discusses Tariffs, AI with WA Small Businesses
item.author: Suzan K. DelBene
item.description:
Democrat · WA · House

…Today, Congresswoman Suzan DelBene (WA-01) joined small business leaders at Shopify's new West Coast port in Bellevue to disuss tariffs and how AI is changing small business commerce. Tariffs drive up operating costs, especially for smaller businesses that operate with thinner margins. Small bu…

…2 months due to tariffs. Washington state is particularly impacted by changes in trade policy, with four-in-ten jobs tied to international commerce. AI is the biggest change in how people buy and sell since the internet began. The growing adoption of this tool presents both challenges and opportuniti…

item.title: RELEASE: Gottheimer Announces Bipartisan “Parents Decide Act” to Protect Kids Online

…ng on self-reported ages. Allow parents to set age-appropriate content controls from the start, including limiting access to social media, apps, and AI platforms. Ensure that age and parental settings securely flow to apps and AI platforms, so content is tailored appropriately for children. Prevent…

…s from the start, including limiting access to social media, apps, and AI platforms. Ensure that age and parental settings securely flow to apps and AI platforms, so content is tailored appropriately for children. Prevent children from accessing harmful or explicit content — including inappropriate…

item.title: Husted joins bill to help small businesses utilize AI

item.description:

…petitive and continue supporting our economy.” WASHINGTON – Sen. Jon Husted (R-Ohio) joined Sens. Todd Young (R-Ind.) and Maria Cantwell’s (D-Wash.) AI for Mainstreet Act. This bill would provide training, guidance and support to American small businesses looking to implement artificial intelligence…

item.title: Senators Kim and Ricketts Introduce MATCH Act to Level the Global Playing Field for U.S. Tech
item.author: Andy Kim
item.description:
Democrat · NJ · Senate

…anufacturing equipment that China desperately needs to build up their own chip industry. If the U.S. is going to maintain a national security edge in AI, quantum computing, and other critical industries, and outcompete the CCP, the MATCH Act is a vital and necessary leap forward,” said Leader Schumer.…

…ation of export controls among allies and closing servicing and entity-specific loopholes, the MATCH Act preserves the U.S. technological lead in the AI competition with China and levels the playing field for American firms. Key provisions of the MATCH Act include: Country-Wide Prohibition on “Choke…

item.title: Ricketts, Kim to Introduce MATCH Act; Level the Global Playing Field for U.S. Tech
item.author: Pete Ricketts
item.description:
Republican · NE · Senate

…ation of export controls among allies and closing servicing and entity-specific loopholes, the MATCH Act preserves the U.S. technological lead in the AI competition with China and eliminates the competitive disadvantage currently facing American firms. “The ability to design and produce semiconductor…

…anufacturing equipment that China desperately needs to build up their own chip industry. If the U.S. is going to maintain a national security edge in AI, quantum computing, and other critical industries, and outcompete the CCP, the MATCH Act is a vital and necessary leap forward,” said Senator Schumer…

…tructure required for their data centers, so those costs aren’t passed onto the hardworking Americans who live nearby. Senator Rick Scott said, “Big Data centers are already putting a substantial financial burden on American families, who are still struggling with the negative impact of four years of Democrat…

West Virginia has a real opportunity in this space, and I am confident that we will equip our next generation wit…

item.title: Murphy, Schatz Introduce Legislation to Reverse Trump's State AI Law Moratorium
item.author: Christopher Murphy
item.description:
Democrat · CT · Senate

…is Murphy (D-Conn.) joined U.S. Senator Brian Schatz (D-Hawai’i) in introducing the Guaranteeing and Upholding Americans’ Right to Decide Responsible AI Laws and Standards (GUARDRAILS) Act to repeal President Trump’s executive order seeking to prevent states from regulating artificial intelligence. Th…

…ht to Decide Responsible AI Laws and Standards (GUARDRAILS) Act to repeal President Trump’s executive order seeking to prevent states from regulating artificial intelligence. The Trump administration renewed its call for a preemption of state-level AI laws as part of its National AI Policy Framework which was released las…

item.author: Elizabeth Warren
item.description:
Democrat · MA · Senate

…riffs jack up prices for American families and further decimate the manufacturing industry. The letter follows a recent episode involving Elon Musk’s AI chatbot Grok, which resulted in the proliferation of millions of dangerous deepfakes, underscoring the danger of Trump’s campaign to advance Big Tech…

…egulatory agenda across the globe,” wrote Senator Warren. On December 29, 2025, Elon Musk’s X unveiled a new feature allowing users to use Grok, X’s AI chatbot, to create and edit images with just one click. This tool sparked a wave of users requesting explicit deepfake images, enabling widespread se…

Senator Brian Schatz (D-Hawai‘i) led a group of six senators in introducing the Guaranteeing and Upholding Americans’ Right to Decide Responsible AI Laws and Standards (GUARDRAILS) Act to repeal President Trump’s executive order seeking to prevent states from regulating artificial intelligence. The stakes go far beyond a single company, with real consequences for the American economy and our national security, especia…

This would be the first time an American company has publicly received this designation, which is gene…

Foundation models are trained on vast and diverse datasets and power many of our generative…

item.title: UNANIMOUS: Bipartisan Huizenga Legislation to Curb AI Chip Smuggling Passes Committee
item.author: Bill Huizenga
item.description:
Republican · MI · House

…technical security measures to detect and prevent smuggling to unauthorized countries and end-users. This legislation responds to ongoing reports of AI chips being smuggled into China, where they are used to power state-controlled AI projects. Despite current export controls, smuggling networks and f…

…ries and end-users. This legislation responds to ongoing reports of AI chips being smuggled into China, where they are used to power state-controlled AI projects. Despite current export controls, smuggling networks and front companies continue to move U.S.-made chips into restricted countries. Last w…

Democrat · IL · House

…putting affordability over profits, deploys the most affordable and reliable energy we have ever had, modernizes our electric grid, and ensures that data centers and large energy users pay their fair share instead of passing those costs onto families and small businesses,” said Rep. Mike Levin. “American fami…

…isn’t shut off Cracking down on price gouging, so energy companies can’t take advantage of you to boost their profits Ensuring that facilities like data centers are paying for their own costs because it’s not fair for their expenses to be pushed onto your household if one opens in your area Giving a voice t…

item.title: Castor Praises Introduction of the Energy Bills Relief Act by SEEC Leaders
item.author: Kathy Castor
item.description:
Democrat · FL · House

…isn’t shut off Cracking down on price gouging, so energy companies can’t take advantage of you to boost their profits Ensuring that facilities like data centers are paying for their own costs because it’s not fair for their expenses to be pushed onto your household if one opens in your area Giving a voice t…
